seoTitle: Brown Range vs Brunswick — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Brown Range and Brunswick. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Brown Range vs Brunswick: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Brown Range and Brunswick.
heading: Brown Range vs Brunswick — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Brown Range, Western Australia and Brunswick, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Brunswick has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Brown Range (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Brown Range or Brunswick safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Brown Range has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Brunswick scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Brown Range scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Brunswick scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Brown Range or Brunswick?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Brunswick has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
